title: Oxyacantha mollis
taxonomy: Oxyacantha mollis is a type of flowering plant, specifically a shrub, and it belongs to the family Rosaceae. More specifically, it is a member of the genus Oxyacantha. However, some sources may categorize it under the genus Rosa as a synonym for Rosa mollis. This classification may be due to the ongoing process of botanical nomenclature and taxonomy. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and current taxonomic understanding.
uses: Oxyacantha mollis, also known as Downy Hawthorn, has been used in traditional medicine for its various properties. The plant's bark and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. In some cultures, Oxyacantha mollis is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its purported ability to calm the stomach and promote digestion. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal teas and infusions to help alleviate stress and promote relaxation. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effects on human health.
